WHY LOCAL MEDIA ADVERTISING GOT HARDER TO SELL
articles.summary: Local media advertising is harder to sell because the mix of local businesses has shifted. New advertisers favor targeted digital tactics over traditional media. The market added 1.7M net new businesses after the pandemic, and many of these buy search ads and targeted banners that sales reps often miss. There are now 3X more in-house marketers than ad sales reps, and these marketers focus on quick results and bottom-funnel tools rather than brand building. This makes advertisers more skeptical and more focused on proof over reach.

articles.title: YOUR 2026 MARKETING BUDGET HAS A HOLE IN IT — AND IT'S CALLED TOKENS
articles.summary: AI token costs are a fast-growing gap in 2026 marketing budgets because usage scales quickly and is hard to predict. Traditional B2B budget splits of about 45% headcount, 45% programs, and 10% technology are shifting as tech spend rises by 2% to 3% due to AI tools and data needs. Headcount is flat at many SaaS companies, but expectations are higher. AI-native companies are hiring as revenue grows faster than staff. Program spend is steady but faces pressure to lower cost-per-outcome with the help of AI. The biggest challenge is forecasting token usage for both internal AI work and vendor pricing, since even providers lack clear benchmarks.

articles.title: EMAIL ANIMATED GIFS: BEST PRACTICES FOR DRIVING ROI WHILE AVOIDING ANNOYING SUBSCRIBERS
articles.summary: Brands using purposeful animated GIFs in email see a 37:1 ROI, a 105% improvement over those relying on static images alone. Interactive elements like gamified reveals and video-GIFs drive 73% higher click-to-open rates by creating a curiosity gap that pulls subscribers into engagement. However, 31% of subscribers label animated emails as annoying when the motion feels random, so every animation needs to serve a clear function. Gmail clips emails over 102KB, and GIFs should stay under 500KB to render properly on mobile.

articles.title: AUGUST PERIOD CARE'S VIRAL COMMUNITY-FIRST MARKETING STRATEGY
articles.summary: August built rapid growth by openly normalizing periods and using a community-first strategy that prioritizes relatability over traditional ads. It has grown to 400K TikTok followers and 187K Instagram followers by using founder-led content and high volume testing that attract and convert Gen Z audiences aged 16-29. It focuses on organic content first and uses paid ads only for retargeting. It scales reach through programs like campus advocates and partnerships with brands like DoorDash and USA Track and Field.

articles.title: INSIDE 5 FOOD AND BEVERAGE INFLUENCER CAMPAIGNS THAT DELIVERED MEASURABLE ROI
articles.summary: Results improve when brands move beyond large creator rosters and toward more deliberate strategic choices. Habit Burger drove 10.5M impressions by deploying food trucks and creators on the ground during Big Game weekend instead of buying a traditional ad spot. Tapatío Ramen integrated direct purchase links into creator content and ran paid amplification on the best-performing assets, achieving 41% higher ROAS and surpassing its own Amazon sales through Shopify. Totino's took a different route, giving TikTok gaming creators minimal creative guardrails and landing a $0.61 CPM across 11.7M impressions.

THE ART OF TELLING STORIES WITH DATA (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: Gulrez Khan will dive into the world of data storytelling in this free event that will take place on April 20 at 9 AM PT. Learn how to transform raw data into compelling narratives that captivate your audience in a world of AI slop. He will cover how to design visuals that guide attention and reduce cognitive load.

articles.title: WHY AI SUCKS AT FRONT END
articles.summary: AI tools excel at scaffolding generic UI components, migrating design tokens, and outlining features. However, they fall apart when front-end work requires pixel precision or bespoke interactions. The core issue is that LLMs trained on common web patterns cannot see rendered output, struggle with math-heavy layout calculations, and have no control over the chaotic browser environment where HTML and CSS actually execute. Scroll-driven animations and complex component states are particularly weak spots. The more complex a component becomes, the slower and less reliable AI assistance gets.

articles.title: COPYWRITING FRIDAY: ONE OF THE FEW THINGS ON THE SPACE SHUTTLE THAT DIDN'T HAVE A BACKUP SYSTEM
articles.summary: Nikon's ad proves reliability by placing its camera in a high-stakes context instead of making generic claims. Using NASA as an authority signal, it shows the camera was trusted on a Space Shuttle without a backup, signaling extreme dependability. Specific details like 1971 and the admission of modifications add credibility. The ad then connects this extreme use case to everyday users, implying the camera will handle normal situations with ease. It focuses on customer outcomes, making the message feel like a logical conclusion rather than a sales claim.

articles.title: AEO IS NOT SEO 2.0
articles.summary: Treating AEO as an upgrade to SEO misunderstands how LLMs work. There are no keywords to rank for and no page one. Responses are personalized based on prompts, history, preferences, and platform. Visibility depends on brand authority and trust signals, not technical optimization alone, though crawlability and structured data still matter for ingestion. The takeaway is to measure brand recognition and third-party mentions, not prompt visibility like a ranking report.

articles.title: AI SEO CAUTIONARY TALE: CLICKUP
articles.summary: ClickUp scaled thousands of AI-optimized listicles beyond its expertise, gaining AI visibility but losing millions in Google traffic.

articles.title: WHY EVERY BRAND NEEDS A NEMESIS
articles.summary: Shared dislike unites people, and that unity turns casual buyers into vocal advocates.
